description: 12x60 terrestrial spotting telescope for sports, boating, bird watching. Objective: 420mm EFL f/7 with 60mm EPD. Porro Type I prism erector. Erfle eyepiece 35mm EFL. 4deg true FOV, 48deg apparent FOV.

prism_erector.description: Two right-angle prisms providing upright image. Modeled as equivalent glass block.

field_corrector.description: Cemented doublet between prism block and image plane to correct off-axis aberrations (astigmatism, field curvature).

eyepiece.description: Wide-angle Erfle eyepiece, designed separately. 35mm EFL at f/7 matches 5mm exit pupil.

assumptions: Porro Type I prism modeled as equivalent N-BK7 glass block of 160mm path length.
Eyepiece is designed separately; this design focuses on the objective + prism + corrector optical train producing a well-corrected intermediate image at the field stop.
True field of view is 4.0° (half-field 2.0°), derived from 48° apparent FOV / 12x magnification.
Visible spectrum: F, d, C lines (standard visual).
Starting point derived from scaling principles — no existing 1200mm design file available; starting from first principles with a Fraunhofer doublet.
